text.title: Vector DB Prod Setup — Failure Modes
text.summary: Five high-impact antipatterns in production vector DB deployment.

failure-mode.description: Ephemeral volume — pod reschedules; all data gone; full re-ingest required.
failure-mode.repair: Switch to PVC with durable storage class. Migrate data before destroying old pod.

failure-mode.description: No resource limits — pod consumes node memory; neighbours evicted; cascading failures.
failure-mode.repair: Set requests + limits. Memory limit ≥ 1.5× expected working set.

failure-mode.description: Single Qdrant replica without snapshot — pod crash means data loss until snapshot restored.
failure-mode.repair: Add backup CronJob (daily). Verify restore in staging.

failure-mode.description: Untested backup — backups exist; restore never tried; restore fails when needed.
failure-mode.detector: backup.last_restore_drill missing OR older than 120 days
failure-mode.repair: Schedule quarterly restore drill. Record outcome + duration.

failure-mode.description: No rollback — bad deploy goes live; no way back; downtime.
failure-mode.detector: deploy strategy missing rollback step in runbook
failure-mode.repair: Use blue/green or canary; keep previous version reachable for 24h.
